text:
In the first space, I am struck by the stillness and starkness. The minimalist design and muted colors make me feel contemplative, almost as if I am awaiting something undefined. The absence of people amplifies a sense of solitude, perhaps inviting introspection about the purpose of such emptiness in shared spaces.

In the second space, color and information abound. The wall is vibrant, filled with text and screens, drawing my attention in many directions. I feel curious and slightly overwhelmed, sensing that there is much to learn about innovation and our relationship with technology. It is a place where ideas compete for dominance, making me aware of the complexity and layered nature of progress.
